paper.title: Revisiting hypervelocity stars after Gaia DR2

extraction.summary: 41 hypervelocity candidates with P_bound<0.5 after Gaia DR2. 30 individually named in Tab. 1 + 11 unnamed 'and others' from HVS Survey. Composition: 38 B/A dwarfs, 1 subdwarf O (US708), 1 F9 dwarf (Li10), 1 white dwarf (GD 492). All candidates were introduced by prior papers; this paper reassesses their bound status with Gaia DR2 astrometry. Per-star astrometry is in the Open Fast Stars Catalog (OFSC), not tabulated here.

method_chain.summary: Open Fast Stars Catalog (524 candidates) automatically queried Gaia DR2. 514 with photometry, 501 with astrometry. Compiled historical data: spectral types, radial velocities, photometry from discovery papers.
outputs: 524 candidates with Gaia DR2 astrometry and photometry

source_refs.context: OFSC: 524 candidates, 514 Gaia photometry, 501 Gaia astrometry
Appendix: OFSC automatically queries Gaia, extinction from Green+2018

method_chain.summary: Bayesian distance inference: Gamma(3, L) prior with spatially-varying L from Bailer-Jones+2018. Photometric distances use Gaussian prior. Gaia parallax offset -0.029 mas applied.

source_refs.context: Appendix: Gamma(3,L) prior, Bailer-Jones+2018 scale-length
Parallax offset -0.029 mas from Lindegren+2018

method_chain.summary: Galactocentric rest-frame velocity v_grf via Johnson & Soderblom (1987). Solar: R0=8.27±0.29 kpc, Vc=238±9 km/s, (U,V,W)=(11.1,12.24,7.25) km/s.

source_refs.context: Appendix: Johnson & Soderblom (1987) transformation, solar/Galactic params

method_chain.summary: Escape velocity: v_esc(r)=v_esc_sun (r/R_sun)^(-α/2). Williams+2017: α=0.37(+0.09/-0.09), v_esc_sun=521.26(+45.79/-30.23) km/s.

source_refs.context: Appendix: Williams+2017 escape velocity parametrization

method_chain.summary: MC: 10^4 emcee samples from multivariate normal + distance prior. P_bound = fraction with v_grf < v_esc(r). Posterior Beta(N_b+1/2, N-N_b+1/2). Missing components → upper limit. 41 with P_bound<0.5.

source_refs.context: Appendix: 10^4 emcee samples, Beta posterior
41 candidates with P_bound<0.5, 428 >0.99 bound
159 in graveyard: bound in all 10^4 samples
Tab. 1: Candidates with P_bound<0.5 listed by survey/paper with names
Tab. 1: 41 candidates with P_bound<0.5, includes B/A dwarf (early-type)

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS1: B/A dwarf (early-type), confirmed with P_bound<0.5 after Gaia DR2 by Boubert et al. (2018). Originally introduced by 9. Late B-type hypervelocity star in Galactic halo. Discovered via radial velocity survey.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper computes P_bound<0.5 using Gaia DR2 astrometry with Monte Carlo sampling of Milky Way escape velocity profile. Individual per-star astrometry is in the Open Fast Stars Catalog (https://faststars.space), not tabulated in the paper.

source_refs.context: 41 candidates with P_bound<0.5 after Gaia DR2
Tab. 1: list of 41 candidates by survey
Appendix: MC sampling, P_bound via Beta(1/2,1/2) interval
Paper re-evaluates 524 previously-known candidates using Gaia DR2

Tab. 1: US708 (a.k.a. HVS2) from Hirsch et al. (2005)

inclusion_assessment.summary: US708: subdwarf O, confirmed with P_bound<0.5 after Gaia DR2 by Boubert et al. (2018). Originally introduced by 10. Helium subdwarf O star. Likely low-mass donor ejected by thermonuclear SN Ia. Fastest known HVS.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper computes P_bound<0.5 using Gaia DR2 astrometry with Monte Carlo sampling of Milky Way escape velocity profile. Individual per-star astrometry is in the Open Fast Stars Catalog (https://faststars.space), not tabulated in the paper.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HE 0437-5439: B/A dwarf, confirmed with P_bound<0.5 after Gaia DR2 by Boubert et al. (2018). Originally introduced by 11. 8 M_sun star thought ejected from the LMC. Located near LMC on sky.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper computes P_bound<0.5 using Gaia DR2 astrometry with Monte Carlo sampling of Milky Way escape velocity profile. Individual per-star astrometry is in the Open Fast Stars Catalog (https://faststars.space), not tabulated in the paper.

Li10 = LAMOST J115209.12+120258.0, late-type HVS candidate

inclusion_assessment.summary: Li10: F9 dwarf, confirmed with P_bound<0.5 after Gaia DR2 by Boubert et al. (2018). Originally introduced by 15. Only confirmed late-type hypervelocity star. v_grf=643±93 km/s. Pericentric radius 3.3±0.2 kpc rules out Hills mechanism. SOAR/Goodman: v_r=234±5 km/s. Disc crossing ~15 Myr ago.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper computes P_bound<0.5 using Gaia DR2 astrometry with Monte Carlo sampling of Milky Way escape velocity profile. Individual per-star astrometry is in the Open Fast Stars Catalog (https://faststars.space), not tabulated in the paper.

Tab. 1: GD 492 (white dwarf) from Vennes et al. (2017)

inclusion_assessment.summary: GD 492: white dwarf, confirmed with P_bound<0.5 after Gaia DR2 by Boubert et al. (2018). Originally introduced by 16. Closest HVS to Sun (d_hel=632±14 pc). Partially burnt remnant of subluminous SN Ia. Low-mass WD with intermediate-element-rich atmosphere.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper computes P_bound<0.5 using Gaia DR2 astrometry with Monte Carlo sampling of Milky Way escape velocity profile. Individual per-star astrometry is in the Open Fast Stars Catalog (https://faststars.space), not tabulated in the paper.

candidate_groups_considered.description: 11 unnamed 'and others' from Hypervelocity Star Survey (Brown 2005, 2007, 2014). Early-type B/A dwarfs with P_bound<0.5 after Gaia DR2. Individual identifiers only available in the Open Fast Stars Catalog (https://faststars.space).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Named only as 'and others' in Table 1. Individual identifiers and per-star data reside in the external OFSC, not in the paper.

source_refs.context: HVS Survey: 32 stars, HVS1,4-10,12-24 and others

candidate_groups_considered.description: 159 previously-claimed hypervelocity candidates confirmed as bound in all 10^4 MC samples, placed in OFSC 'graveyard'. 428 have P_bound>0.99. Mostly late-type FGKM stars ruled out by Gaia DR2.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: These stars are confirmed bound to the Milky Way by Gaia DR2. 428 ruled out at >99% confidence, 159 bound in every single MC sample.

source_refs.context: 159 candidates in OFSC graveyard: bound in all 10^4 samples
428 with P_bound>0.99 ruled out
